data.title: Blackstone Q2 earnings top consensus, helped by growth in private wealth, credit and insurance
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) Q2 earnings grew from the prior quarter and a year ago, beating the Wall Street consensus and reflecting the company's expansion, especially in private wealth, credit and insurance, and infrastructure.

Total assets under management of $1.211T at June 30, 2025, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.196T, rose from $1.168T at March 31, 2025. Fee-earning AUM increased to $887.1B from $860.1B at the end of Q1 2025.

Net inflows of $52.1B compared with inflows of $61.6B in the prior quarter. 

"Despite the volatile market backdrop, we grew earnings significantly and total assets under management increased 13% year-over-year to more than $1.2 trillion—a new industry record," Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman said.

Q2 distributable EPS of $1.21, vs. the consensus of $1.10, increased from $1.09 in Q1 and $0.96 in last year’s Q2.

Blackstone (NYSE:BX) stock rose 2.4% in Thursday premarket trading.

Q2 fee-related earnings of $1.46B, or $1.19 per share, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.36B, rose from $1.26B, or $1.03 per share, in Q1 and $1.11B, or $0.91 per share, in last year’s Q2.

Net management and advisory fees were $2.02B, up from $1.89B in the previous quarter and $1.78B a year ago.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $181.2B vs. $177.2B at the end of Q1. During the first quarter of 2025, the company deployed $33.1B of capital and recorded realizations of $23.4B.

Total expenses of $1.93B vs. $1.89B in Q1 and $1.63B in Q2 2024. Compensation and benefit expenses dipped to $1.42B from $1.43B in the prior quarter and climbed from $1.21B a year ago.

Q1 investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate

    • Opportunistic: +0.1%

    • Core: -0.4% 

  • Private Equity

    • Corporate Private Equity: +5.1%

    • Tactical Opportunities: +4.1%

    • Secondaries: +6.6%

    • Infrastructure: +2.9%

  • Credit & Insurance

    • Private Credit: +3.0%

    • Liquid Credit: +2.4%

  • Multi-Asset Investing

    • Absolute Return Composite: +2.8%

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

data.title: Laboratorios Farmaceuticos Rovi, S.A. reports H1 results
data.description:
• Laboratorios Farmaceuticos Rovi, S.A. press release (OTCPK:LABFF): H1 Net profit amounted to 39.7 million euros in the first half of 2025.
  • Operating revenue in the first half of 2025 was 314.6 million euros, a 4% decrease on the first half of 2024.
  • OUTLOOK: For 2025, ROVI expects its operating revenue to decrease by a mid-single-digit percentage in comparison with 2024. Notwithstanding, this guidance is calculated using certain factors that, although they could be relevant to the estimates, are difficult to specify at the present time, such as how the demand and production might evolve for the vaccination campaigns that will take place in 2025.

data.title: Civista Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.01, revenue of $41.4M misses by $1.18M
data.description:
• Civista Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:CIVB): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.71 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $41.4M (+8.6% Y/Y) misses by $1.18M.
  • Efficiency ratio of 64.5%, compared to 72.6% for the second quarter of 2024 and 64.9% for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Net interest margin (tax equivalent) of 3.64%, compared to 3.09% for the second quarter of 2024.
  • Total period end loan growth of $47.1 million from first quarter 2025.
  • Return on Assets of 1.06%, compared to 0.72% for the second quarter of 2024.
  • Return on Equity of 11.02%, compared to 7.77% for the second quarter of 2024.
  • Allowance for credit losses on loans / total loans of 1.28%.
  • Total deposits at June 30, 2025, were $3.2 billion, a decrease of $42.7 million, or 1.32% from March 31, 2025, and a decrease of $15.7 million, or 0.5%, from December 31, 2024.  
  • Provision for credit losses (including provision for unfunded commitments) decreased $0.7 million for the second quarter of 2025 to $1.0 million compared to $1.7 million for the same period last year, and decreased $0.6 million compared to $1.6 million in the first quarter of 2025.

data.title: Newmont suspends operations at British Columbia mine where workers trapped
data.description:
Newmont (NYSE:NEM) -0.9% pre-market Thursday, as rescue efforts are continuing at its Red Chris gold and copper mine in British Columbia where three workers are trapped underground.

The workers were trapped after two incidents in which material unexpectedly fell and blocked access at a "non-producing project" within the mine on Tuesday, the company said.

The first fall of ground prompted three employees to relocate to a designated refuge station, and a second fall subsequently blocked the access route and restricted communication.

Newmont (NYSE:NEM) has temporarily suspended operations at Red Chris, which produced nearly 40K oz of gold last year, making it one of the company's smaller operations; Newmont owns a 70% in the mine and operates it in a joint venture with Imperial Metals (OTCPK:IPMLF).

data.title: Dime Community Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.64 beats by $0.02, revenue of $109.7M beats by $3.25M
data.description:
• Dime Community Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:DCOM): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.64 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $109.7M (+25.6% Y/Y) beats by $3.25M.
  • Total deposits increased $711.7 million on a year-over-year basis;
  • Core deposits (excluding brokered and time deposits) increased $1.21 billion on a year-over-year basis;
  • The ratio of average non-interest-bearing deposits to average total deposits for the second quarter was 30%;
  • Business loans grew $113.3 million on a linked quarter basis and $371.3 million on a year-over-year basis;
  • The net interest margin increased to 2.98% for the second quarter of 2025 compared to 2.95% for the prior quarter; and
  • The Company’s Common Equity Tier 1 Ratio increased to 11.25% at the end of the second quarter.

data.title: Becle, S.A.B. de C.V. reports Q2 results

  • net sales increased 2.8% year-over-year to P$11,462 million, supported by positive currency translation effects.
  • During the second quarter of 2025, total volume decreased 4.2% to 6,458 million nine-liter cases.

data.title: Dow shares tumble as company swings to loss, slashes dividend amid global weakness
data.description:
Dow (NYSE:DOW) shares dropped 8.3% in premarket trading Thursday after the chemicals giant reported a steep second-quarter loss, deteriorating margins and a surprise dividend cut, highlighting growing pressure from persistent global demand weakness and intensifying trade tensions.

The company posted a GAAP net loss of $801 million, or $1.18 a share, compared to a $458 million profit in the same quarter last year, and the analysts’ estimate of a loss of $0.19 a share. On an adjusted basis, operating earnings per share came in at a loss of $0.42, marking a sharp reversal from a $0.68 gain a year ago, and missing the consensus for a loss of $0.17.

Revenue fell 7% year-over-year to $10.1 billion, missing the Wall Street estimate of $10.23 billion, as local prices declined across all regions and segments. Volume slipped 1%, dragged by lower demand in Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and India, offsetting modest gains in the U.S. and Canada.

Cash flow also deteriorated sharply. Dow reported negative cash flow from operating activities of $470 million, down more than $1.3 billion from the same period last year.

In a move that rattled investors, Dow cut its dividend, citing a need to “maintain a balanced capital allocation framework” during what it called a “lower-for-longer earnings environment.”

“We are delivering near-term cash support and earnings growth levers, which we anticipate will total more than $6 billion by 2026,” Jim Fitterling, chair and chief executive, said in a statement. “We are also adjusting our dividend to ensure we maintain a balanced capital allocation framework. We remain committed to targeting a competitive dividend across the economic cycle.”

Segment struggles, restructuring effects

The company’s Packaging & Specialty Plastics segment, its largest by revenue, saw a 9% year-over-year sales decline to $5 billion. Operating EBIT for the segment fell dramatically to $71 million, down from $703 million a year ago, as lower polymer prices and the ramp-up of Dow’s (NYSE:DOW) new Poly-7 polyethylene facility hurt margins.

Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure reported a deeper operating loss of $185 million, down from a modest $7 million gain in the comparable period a year earlier, hurt by lower prices and equity losses in joint ventures, including Sadara and operations in Kuwait.

The Performance Materials & Coatings division was the lone relative bright spot, with sales falling a modest 5% and operating profit rising slightly to $152 million. The gain was driven by lower input costs and seasonal demand for coatings and silicones.

Dow also recorded $591 million in restructuring and asset-related charges, tied to a broader portfolio realignment in Europe and workforce reductions under its 2025 restructuring program. Excluding significant items, the operating loss narrowed to $191 million.

Outlook: Macro headwinds, trade tensions

Fitterling flagged rising concerns over “anti-competitive economics” from global competitors and called for “broader industry engagement and regulatory action” to restore fair market dynamics.

While Dow (DOW) expects its recent growth investments, such as the Poly-7 facility and a new alkoxylation unit in Texas, to improve its downstream margins and product mix, management acknowledged the near-term outlook remains fraught with uncertainty.

“Signs of oversupply” and soft global demand remain challenges, Fitterling said. The company is “focused on structurally improving our cost base, optimizing our global asset footprint and maintaining our track record of operational excellence to further strengthen our competitive advantages.”

data.title: LKQ reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:

  • Revenue of $3.64B (-1.9% Y/Y) beats by $20M.
  • Cash flow from operations and free cash flow1 were $296 million and $243 million, respectively, for the second quarter of 2025. 

  • For 2025, management updated the outlook as set forth below:

      2025 Previous Full YearOutlook 2025 Updated Full YearOutlook
